task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing the contents of /home/ga/build-project/.dockerignore with '.git' and '__pycache__' listed, or terminal output of 'cat .dockerignore' showing these entries.

task_completion.description: Optimize Dockerfile to run apt-get before copying source code.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing /home/ga/build-project/Dockerfile where the 'RUN apt-get ...' command appears before the 'COPY . .' (or similar command that copies the whole source directory).

task_completion.description: Optimize Dockerfile to copy requirements.txt before running pip install.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing /home/ga/build-project/Dockerfile where the 'COPY requirements.txt ...' command appears before the 'RUN pip install ...' command.

task_completion.description: Optimize Dockerfile to run pip install before copying the rest of the source code.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing /home/ga/build-project/Dockerfile where the 'RUN pip install ...' command appears before the 'COPY . .' (or similar command that copies the whole source directory).

task_completion.description: Build the optimized image tagged as 'build-project:optimized'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing the execution of 'docker build -t build-project:optimized .' (or similar) and the build completing successfully without errors.

task_completion.description: Verify the container runs correctly on port 5000.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing 'docker run' with port mapping to 5000 and the container starting, or a successful 'curl localhost:5000' command, or a web browser showing the application running on port 5000.

integrity.description: The agent actually modified the Dockerfile and .dockerignore files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a text editor (like nano, vim, or VS Code) being used to edit the files, or valid terminal commands (like echo or sed) modifying them, rather than just printing the expected output.

integrity.description: The agent actually ran the docker build command.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal shows the actual Docker build process with layer outputs, not just a mock script or text file.

integrity.description: The agent actually tested the container or image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal shows the container running or a curl command returning a real response from the Flask app, not just a simulated output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project directory is /home/ga/build-project. The target image tag must be 'build-project:optimized'. The container must expose and run on port 5000. The Dockerfile optimizations require: 1) apt-get runs before source copy, 2) COPY requirements.txt runs before pip install, 3) pip install runs before source copy. The .dockerignore file must exist and contain both '.git' and '__pycache__'.
